agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $390,000 contract amendment for electric bikeshare program
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility for up to $390,000 to keep the citywide electric bikeshare program running through the end of the fiscal year. This would bring the total contract to $3,540,341. The extra money would come from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget. The purpose is to buy time while the city decides the program's long-term future during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process. This item was continued from the April 28, 2026 meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the bikeshare program continues; the future of the program will be decided in the 2026-27 budget process.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.3
Contract Amendment No. 3 with Charleston Mobility for Electric Bikeshare Operations
[image]  
APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, meeting.
•

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on updated sidewalk vendor rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed update to the city's sidewalk vendor regulations (Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code). The proposed changes would update how vendors apply for permits, set new rules for when fees or requirements can be waived, and strengthen how the city enforces the rules. This is a first reading of the ordinance — it has not been approved yet. The item was carried over from the April 28, 2026 meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, these changes could affect your sidewalk vending licence and operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Introduce Ordinance Amending Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42)
[image]  
INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, meeting.
•

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on resolution opposing CO2 pipelines
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution opposing the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub. This project would involve building a large network of CO2 pipelines through Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties to transport and dump carbon waste underground. Council will consider a resolution that formally opposes these pipelines and the waste dumping project, citing risks to the health and safety of Richmond residents. A presentation on the proposal will be given before the vote. This item was carried over from the April 28, 2026 meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the council meeting or contact Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565) to voice your concerns before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.1
Opposition to Proposed CO2 Waste Dumping Projects & CO2 Pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties
[image]  
RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, meeting.
•

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on appeals of Marina Point residential project approval
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will hold a public hearing to consider two appeals of the Planning Commission's conditional approval of the Marina Point Residential Project (PLN23-117). Council is set to vote on a resolution that would modify the conditions of approval in response to the appeals, while otherwise affirming the original decision. This is a proposed decision — nothing has been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your support or concerns about the Marina Point project before Council makes a final decision.

key_dates.label: Marina Point Residential Project public hearing — Richmond Council

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.1
Appeal of Planning Commission Conditional Approval of Marina Point Residential Project
[image]  
•  
HOLD a public hearing to consider two appeals of the Planning Commission’s conditional approval of the Marina Point Residential Project (PLN23-117) and ADOPT a resolution Modifying the Conditions in response to the appeals and otherwise affirming the Planning Commission’s conditional approval -  Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/ Avery Stark 510-620-6714).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond FY 2026-27 operating budget and capital plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to review a draft budget for the 2026-27 fiscal year and a five-year capital improvement plan (2026-2031). The draft includes proposed spending on city operations and major projects. Council will hear from city departments and discuss the plan before voting on whether to approve it. This is not a final decision — the budget is still a proposal and could change before adoption.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — the final budget could affect city fees, permits, and services your business relies on.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.1
Draft Fiscal Year (FY) 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and FY 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan
[image]  
•  
ACKNOWLEDGE receipt of the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and Fiscal Year 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan; REVIEW the budget adoption schedule; and RECEIVE an update on departmental operations, with discussions upon completion of all departmental presentations – City Manager’s Office/Finance Department (Shasa Curl/Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader 510-412-2077).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Sole-source contract for Ferry Point Pump Station engineering
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a sole-source contract with Schaaf & Wheeler Consulting Civil Engineers for $29,690.43. This contract covers completed construction-related engineering services for the Ferry Point Pump Station Project. The term would run until January 1, 2028. Council is also set to vote on setting aside $45,000 from the Wastewater Enterprise Fund to cover final project costs. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you at this time. Watch for the council decision — this contract affects public works, not private businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.a
Contract with Schaaf & Wheeler Consulting Civil Engineers for the Ferry Point Pump Station Project
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a sole-source contract with Schaaf & Wheeler Consulting Civil Engineers for $29,690.43 for a term ending on January 1, 2028, for completed construction- related engineering services and APPROPRIATE $45,000 from the Wastewater Enterprise Fund for the Ferry Point Pump Station Project final expenditures – Public Works Department (Darcie DeLashmutt 510-672-3944/Tawfic Halaby 510-620-5482/Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $300K community prosecutor deal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed five-year agreement with the Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office. If approved, the deal would dedicate a deputy district attorney (a lawyer who works for the county to prosecute crimes) to serve as Richmond's Community-based Prosecutor. The cost would not exceed $300,000, covering the period from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this could mean more focused prosecution of local crimes affecting your business.

key_dates.label: Proposed community prosecutor agreement start date
Proposed community prosecutor agreement end date

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.g
Agreement with Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office to Continue the Community-based Prosecution Program
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a five-year agreement with the Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office for the dedicated services of a deputy district attorney who would be specifically assigned as Richmond's Community-based Prosecutor, in an amount not to exceed $300,000, for the term commencing July 1, 2026, and ending June 30, 2029 - Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Sole-source mental health contract for Richmond police up to $525,000
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a sole-source contract (a contract awarded without competitive bidding) with Public Safety Family Counseling Group for mental health counseling and consultant services for the Richmond Police Department. The proposed three-year contract, starting July 1, 2026, would cost up to $525,000 total, with an annual limit of $175,000. The contract also includes two optional one-year extensions. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.

key_dates.label: Proposed contract start date — Richmond Police mental health services
Proposed contract end date — Richmond Police mental health services

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.f
Contract with Public Safety Family Counseling Group for Mental Health Counseling and Consultant Services
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a sole-source contract with Public Safety Family Counseling Group, Inc. for mental health counseling and consultant services in an amount not to exceed $525,000, for a three-year term from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, with two optional one-year extensions, for an annual amount not to exceed $175,000 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802/Captain Matthew Stonebraker 510-621-1204).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $90K police background check contracts
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving contracts with T Jung Investigations and Steve Blanc Investigations to conduct pre-employment background checks for police department applicants. Each contract would be worth up to $90,000 over three years, with the option to extend for two additional one-year terms. The contracts are on an as-needed basis, meaning the city only pays for work actually done. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.e
Contract with T Jung Investigations and Steve Blanc Investigations for Pre-Employment Background Investigators
[image]  
•  
APPROVE contracts with T Jung Investigations and, separately, Steve Blanc Investigations, to provide background investigations for Police Department applicants on an as-needed basis, in an amount not to exceed $90,000 per vendor over a three-year contract term of April 15, 2026, to April 15, 2029, with two one- year extension options – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $7.2M animal control contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services for animal control services. The agreement would authorize up to $7,200,000 for the 2026–2027 fiscal year, with an option to extend for three more years (2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030). The total contract would run from July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030. This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this contract will affect animal control services in Richmond.

key_dates.label: Proposed animal control contract vote — Richmond council meeting

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.d
Agreement with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department for Ongoing Animal Control Services
[image]  
•  
APPROVE and AUTHORIZE a contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department in an amount not to exceed $7,200,000 for the continuation of Animal Control Services for Fiscal Year 2026–2027, with an additional three-year term for Fiscal Years 2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030, for a total contract term of July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive March 2026 Richmond Police crime report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to receive the monthly crime report from the Richmond Police Department for March 2026. This is a proposed step — the report will be presented for information only, not for a vote or decision. The report will give council members an update on recent crime trends and police activity in the city. Business owners should watch for the report's release to understand any local crime patterns that could affect their operations or safety.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.c
March 2026 Crime Report
[image]  
•  
RECEIVE the monthly Crime Report from the Richmond Police Department for March 2026 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive February 2026 Richmond crime report
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to receive the monthly crime report from the Richmond Police Department for February 2026. This is a routine report that summarizes recent crime statistics and trends in the city. Council will consider the information as part of their regular oversight of public safety. The report has not yet been approved or enacted — it is simply being presented for council's information and discussion.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.b
Monthly Crime Report for February 2026
[image]  
•  
RECEIVE the monthly Crime Report from the Richmond Police Department for February 2026 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Police car rental deal with Enterprise up to $540,000
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed three-year contract with Enterprise Holdings Incorporated for the Richmond Police Department to rent unmarked police vehicles. The contract would cost up to $540,000 from May 6, 2026, through May 5, 2029, with the option for two one-year extensions that could add another $360,000. This is not yet approved — it is an item on the council agenda for discussion and a vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners — this is an internal police department contract. Just watch for the council decision.

key_dates.label: Proposed Enterprise rental contract start date — Richmond Police
Proposed Enterprise rental contract end date — Richmond Police

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.a
Contract with Enterprise Holdings Incorporated for Rental Car Services
[image]  
•  
AUTHORIZE and APPROVE a three-year contract with Enterprise Holdings Incorporated for the Richmond Police Department to rent unmarked police vehicles for an amount not to exceed $540,000 for the period of May 6, 2026, through May 5, 2029, with two one-year, mutually agreed upon annual extensions for an additional total of $360,000 — Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to appoint Courtney Sanders to ECIA Transportation Board
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposal from the Mayor's Office to appoint Courtney Sanders to a vacant seat on the ECIA Transportation Board. If approved, the term would run until July 25, 2029. This is a proposed appointment that has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.b
Appointment of Courtney Sanders to the ECIA Transportation Board
[image]  
•  
APPOINT Courtney Sanders to a vacant seat on the ECIA Transportation Board with a term that expires on July 25, 2029 – Mayor’s Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve $1.5M radio purchase from Motorola Solutions
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to approve a standing purchase order with Motorola Solutions, Inc. for portable and mobile radios, up to a total of $1,500,000. The agreement would run until May 6, 2029, with the option to extend it twice for one year each, at an additional cost not to exceed $250,000. This purchase is for the Fire Department. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider this at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — this is an internal City procurement decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.3.a
Purchase of Portable and Mobile Radios from Motorola Solutions, Inc.
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a standing purchase order with Motorola Solutions, Inc. to purchase portable and mobile radios in an amount not to exceed $1,500,000 for a term ending May 6, 2029, with the possibility of two one-year extension options in a not to exceed $250,000 – Fire Department (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021/Chief Rico Rincon 510-307-8041/Den Mark Marcelo 510-307-8038).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $69,000 for Richmond Sister Cities delegations
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving $69,000 from the General Fund to support delegation trips to Richmond's sister cities: Zhoushan, China; Regla, Cuba; and Sebastia, Palestine. If approved, the money would pay for travel costs and a scholarship program for participants. The city manager would also be authorized to hire a third-party administrator to manage the funds. This is a proposed decision — it has not been voted on yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.2.b
Appropriate Funding for Sister Cities Commission Partner City Delegations
[image]  
•  
APPROPRIATE $69,000 in General Fund to the Sister Cities Commission to support delegation activities with Zhoushan, China; Regla, Cuba; and Sebastia, Palestine; AUTHORIZE the city manager, with approval as to form by the City Attorney’s Office, to enter into agreement(s) with qualified third-party administrator(s) and take all necessary actions to administer appropriated funds in support of eligible costs associated with the Richmond– Zhoushan Sister City program; and AUTHORIZE city staff to develop and implement a scholarship program, in collaboration with the Zhoushan Sister City Commission, to support travel costs – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Nickie Mastay/Shasa Curl 510-620-6740).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive March 2026 financial reports on investments, overtime, taxes, and pensions
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will consider receiving several financial reports for March 2026, including the Investment and Cash Balance Report, Monthly Overtime Reports, Documentary Transfer Tax Report, and Other Post-Employment Benefits (OPEB) and Pension Report. These reports are proposed for acceptance at an upcoming council meeting and have not yet been approved. The reports provide a snapshot of the city's cash and investments, employee overtime costs, property transfer tax revenue, and long-term benefit obligations for retirees.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.2.a
Investment and Cash Balance Report, Monthly Overtime Reports, Documentary Transfer Tax Report, and Other Post-Employment Benefits (OPEB) and Pension Report for the month of March 2026.
[image]  
•  
RECEIVE the City’s Investment and Cash Balance Report, Monthly Overtime Reports, Documentary Transfer Tax Report, and Other Post-Employment Benefits (OPEB) and Pension Report for the month of March 2026 – Finance Department (Emily Combs 510-620-6740).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to add caste as protected category in municipal code
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance that would add caste as a protected category in the Richmond Municipal Code. If approved, this change would make it illegal to discriminate against someone based on their caste under the city's human rights rules. The proposed amendment would update sections 3.58.010, 3.58.020, and 3.58.060 of the code, giving the city's Human Rights and Human Relations Commission the power to handle caste-based discrimination complaints. This is a second reading of the ordinance, meaning it has been introduced once before and council will now decide whether to adopt it.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this will affect all Richmond businesses and their anti-discrimination policies.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.d
Establishing a Commitment to Caste Equity and the Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination
[image]  
ADOPT an ordinance (second reading) amending sections 3.58.010, 3.58.020, and 3.58.060 of the Richmond Municipal Code to add caste as a protected category within the jurisdiction of the City’s Human Rights and Human Relations Commission – City Attorney’s Office (Shannon Moore 510-620-6505/Dallae Chin 510-620-5517/Brandi Robinson 510-620-5518).
•

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $150,000 legal contract extension
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed fifth amendment to a legal services agreement with the law firm Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP. If approved, the City would spend an additional $150,000 (for a total contract of up to $400,000) and extend the agreement until December 31, 2027. The firm would continue to represent the City in municipal law matters, including personnel issues and lawsuits. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners — this is an internal City legal contract. Watch for the council decision if you follow municipal affairs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.c
Legal Service Agreement Amendment No. 5 with Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP (Burke) for Municipal Litigation Matters
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a fifth amendment to the Legal Services Agreement with Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP, in the amount of $150,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $400,000, and extend the term to December 31, 2027, to continue representing the City in municipal law matters, including personnel matters and litigation – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $500,000 legal services deal
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed legal services agreement with the law firm Bennett, Gelini and Gelini, APC (BGG). If approved, the agreement would pay BGG up to $500,000 to represent the city in lawsuits and handle liability claims. The proposed contract would run from May 5, 2026, to June 30, 2028. This is not yet approved — Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.b
Legal Services Agreement with Bennett, Gelini and Gelini, APC (BGG) for Representation and Liability Claim
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a new legal services agreement with Bennett, Gelini and Gelini, APC, for litigation representation and liability claims in an amount not to exceed $500,000 with a term of May 5, 2026, through June 30, 2028 – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/ Shannon Moore 510-620-6505).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $100K legal services deal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a legal services agreement with the firm Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC. The agreement would cover general litigation (court cases) and municipal advice (legal guidance for the city) for up to $100,000. The proposed term runs from May 5, 2026, through December 31, 2027. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

Legal Services Agreement with Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC for General Litigation and Municipal Advice
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a legal services agreement with Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC, for general litigation and municipal advice in an amount not to exceed $100,000, for a term of May 5, 2026, through December 31, 2027 – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/ Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).
